summary: Split the doctor probe into two independent checks: reachability hits GET / (any HTTP response = reachable, including Nylas's documented 401 at the root) and credentials hit GET /v3/grants?limit=1 (200 = valid, 401 = invalid, 403 = scoped). Previously both probes targeted /v3/grants/me, which requires a grant-scoped token and returned 401 for API-key (admin/account-level) auth even when the key was fully valid.
reason: Direct user report (amend run, 2026-05-19): every new user running `nylas-pp-cli doctor` with a valid API-key hit a false `FAIL Credentials: invalid (HTTP 401)` because /v3/grants/me requires a grant-scoped token, not an API-key, while all other commands (`grants get-all`, etc.) succeeded with the same key. Reproduces on first install, leads users into credential-rotation rabbit holes when nothing is wrong. The fix uses an API-key-compatible authenticated endpoint (/v3/grants list) for the credential verdict and isolates the transport-only reachability probe.
